item.title: UK Says Iran, China Drive Regular Significant Cyberattacks
item.description: Hackers linked to foreign governments are now behind the majority of “nationally significant” cyberattacks targeting the UK, the country’s top cybersecurity official is set to warn.

item.title: Memory Stock Valuations Spark Debate Over ‘Supercycle’ Potential
item.description: Memory makers are riding surging demand to record profits, yet their stocks are still trading at a fraction of the valuation multiples of other top artificial intelligence chip names.

item.title: Unravelling Hedge Fund Trade Derails Boom in Taiwan Convertibles
item.description: A lucrative hedge fund trade in Taiwanese convertible bonds has been derailed by regulatory changes, disrupting issuance in one of the hottest capital markets for the island’s tech sector.

item.title: BHP Says It Has Concluded Iron Ore Sales Negotiations With CMRG
item.description: BHP Group said it has finalized a supply agreement with China’s state-backed iron ore buyer, ending a tense standoff that had led to product restrictions and roiled the market.

item.title: Men’s Wearhouse Owner Files Confidentially for IPO
item.description: Tailored Brands Inc. filed confidentially for an initial public offering, positioning the menswear company to return to public markets later this year after filing for bankruptcy during the pandemic.

item.title: Trump and Tillis in Battle of Wills With Warsh’s Fate in Balance
item.description: The biggest question facing Kevin Warsh was answered before he even reached the committee room Tuesday when President Donald Trump doubled down on a criminal probe that is effectively blocking his own nominee from taking over at the Federal Reserve.

item.title: SpaceX Has Deal for Right to Acquire Cursor for $60 Billion
item.description: SpaceX said it has an agreement to either acquire AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion later this year or pay $10 billion for its work together, as it works to catch up to rivals in AI coding.

item.title: Gold Steadies as Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire, Peace Talks Stall
item.description: Gold held a two-day decline after US President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran and plans for a fresh round of peace talks fell apart.

item.title: US Stock Futures Rise as Trump Extends Iran Truce: Markets Wrap
item.description: US stock futures climbed after President Donald Trump said he’s extending the ceasefire deal with Iran until talks conclude. Oil held a two-day gain.

item.title: CVC, GTCR Weigh Take-Private of Medical Equipment Provider Teleflex
item.description: CVC Capital Partners Plc and GTCR are exploring a takeover of medical equipment provider Teleflex Inc., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Oil Holds Advance as Trump Extends Truce But Maintains Blockade
item.description: Oil held a two-day gain after US President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran, even as peace talks faltered and a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz kept flows locked up.

item.title: Intuitive Surgical Raises 2026 Outlook, Buoyed by US, Europe
item.description: Intuitive Surgical Inc. raised its fiscal 2026 outlook for use of its flagship surgical robotic system and reported first-quarter results that beat expectations, lifted by growing usage in the US and Europe.

item.title: Optiver Taps Former Citadel Trader for Options Expansion
item.description: Optiver has recruited Lawrence Tanzman from Citadel Securities as the Dutch market maker expands into handling options trades sent by retail brokerages, a business long dominated by the Ken Griffin-owned firm.

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Model Is Being Accessed by Unauthorized Users
item.description: A small group of unauthorized users have accessed Anthropic PBC’s new Mythos AI model, a technology that the company says is so powerful it can enable dangerous cyberattacks, according to a person familiar with the matter and documentation viewed by Bloomberg News.

item.title: Housing REIT National Healthcare’s US IPO Raises $462 Million
item.description: National Healthcare Properties Inc., a real estate investment trust, raised $462 million in ah initial public offering priced below the marketed range.

item.title: Vanguard Scoops Up Treasuries as Iran Conflict Lifts Yields
item.description: Vanguard is boosting its holdings of Treasuries, taking advantage of higher yields following the Middle East conflict to lock in rates and hedge against the risks of a potential growth slowdown.

item.title: NASA Unveils Powerful Space Telescope Ahead of September Launch
item.description: NASA unveiled its next-generation space observatory on Tuesday, the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, now fully assembled ahead of its planned launch as early as this fall.

item.title: Apple’s Tim Cook Takes On Crucial New Role: Global Ambassador
item.description: Now that Tim Cook is shedding the yoke of running Apple Inc., he can devote more time to an increasingly crucial role: acting as the company’s global ambassador.

item.title: Commodity Traders Reap Profit Bonanza as Iran War Upends Markets
item.description: The world’s biggest energy traders are reaping a fresh profit bonanza as the war in Iran causes unprecedented disruption to oil markets.

item.title: BOJ Watchers Now See June Rate Hike as Iran War Pushes Back Bets
item.description: With Donald Trump’s war in Iran diminishing expectations for a Bank of Japan interest-rate hike next week, a majority of economists are now betting the next increase will come in June, according to a survey by Bloomberg News.

item.title: Top Law Firm Apologizes to Bankruptcy Judge for AI Hallucination
item.description: One of Wall Street’s prominent law firms, Sullivan & Cromwell, wrote to a bankruptcy judge to apologize for a court motion that included inaccurate citations generated by artificial intelligence, according to a filing in the US Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of New York.

item.title: Bond Traders Bet on Post-War Calm to Keep Rates in Tight Range
item.description: Traders are building up bets that bond market volatility will keep falling, even as a US-Iran peace agreement remains elusive.

item.title: Capital One Boosts Provision for Bad Loans, Misses Estimates
item.description: Capital One Financial Corp., the biggest US credit-card lender, reported a first-quarter profit that missed Wall Street estimates and set aside more cash to cover soured loans.

item.title: Adobe Announces $25 Billion Buyback Following Share Slide
item.description: Adobe Inc. will buy back as much as $25 billion of its stock following a steady decline for more than two years over investor concerns that artificial intelligence will disrupt the company’s business.

item.title: Roblox Settles With States For $35.8 Million Over Child Safety
item.description: Roblox Corp. has reached settlements with attorneys general in three states over child-safety protections.

item.title: China’s Tech Grip Threatens India’s Ambitions
item.description: On today’s Big Take Asia podcast, we look at India’s ambitious manufacturing dream and why it can’t achieve it without China.

item.title: Tillis Signals Openness to Warsh Vote If Congress Adds Fed Probe
item.description: Republican Senator Thom Tillis signaled openness to advancing Kevin Warsh’s nomination to be Federal Reserve chair if an ongoing US criminal inquiry into the central bank is dropped and Congress launches its own investigation.

item.title: OpenAI Unveils New Image Model That’s Better at Charts and Diagrams
item.description: OpenAI is releasing an update to its artificial intelligence image-generating software that it says will let users create accurate, complex charts and scientific diagrams, part of a bid by the company to make its technology more appealing to professionals.

item.title: Top Oil Traders Say Billion-Barrel Shock to Echo Long After War
item.description: The impact of the Iran war will continue for months even after any deal to restore shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, the world’s largest oil traders have warned. Some said flows through the waterway may never return to normal.

item.title: Paraguay Leaves Key Rate at 5.5% on Stable Inflation Outlook
item.description: Paraguay’s central bank held its benchmark interest rate unchanged for a second-consecutive month at 5.5% amid a stable inflation outlook.
